channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: The Grateful Dead Launches Play Dead Streaming Platform: ‘We Want to Melt Your Faces’
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest music trends. Today, we're talking about a game-changer for Grateful Dead fans. Get ready to explore the ultimate live music experience with Play Dead, a new app dedicated to the iconic band's extensive live catalog."
[Short music transition]
Host: "The Grateful Dead has been a cultural phenomenon for decades, with fans trading live recordings since the 1980s. Now, thanks to a partnership between nugs, Grateful Dead Productions, and Rhino Entertainment, fans can access high-resolution streaming of over 300 concerts, including many previously only available on physical releases."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Play Dead subscriptions start at $9.99/month or $99.99/year, with special pricing options for existing nugs subscribers. But here's the exciting part: the service will release two new, previously unreleased vault recordings every week, forever. That's right, an exponential increase in new archival releases for Deadheads."
[Short music transition]
Host: "We spoke with nugs founder and CEO Brad Serling, a self-proclaimed Deadhead who's been working towards this moment for decades. He says, 'This is my life's work... I was born to do this.' And it's been a long time coming, with Serling first discussing the idea with the band back in 2000."
[Outro music plays]
Host: "So, if you're a Grateful Dead fan, get ready to dive into the ultimate live music experience. Play Dead is now available, and it's a must-have for any Deadhead. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next episode."
[Outro music continues to play until the end]
